description: Amazon's 16 Leadership Principles are central to every interview. Behavioral questions are explicitly mapped to LPs. Interviewers are assigned specific LPs to evaluate.

leadership_principles.name: Customer Obsession
leadership_principles.description: Leaders start with the customer and work backwards.
interview_probes: Tell me about a time you prioritized the customer over short-term business goals
How do you gather and use customer feedback?
positive_signals: Has concrete examples of customer-driven decisions
negative_signals: Optimizes for convenience over customer value

leadership_principles.name: Ownership
leadership_principles.description: Leaders are owners. They think long term and don't sacrifice long-term value for short-term results. They act on behalf of the entire company, beyond just their own team.
interview_probes: Tell me about a time you took on something outside your area of responsibility
Describe a time you saw a problem and fixed it without being asked

leadership_principles.name: Invent and Simplify
leadership_principles.description: Leaders expect and require innovation and invention from their teams and always find ways to simplify.
interview_probes: Tell me about a time you found a simple solution to a complex problem
Describe an innovative approach you took

leadership_principles.name: Are Right, A Lot
leadership_principles.description: Leaders are right a lot. They have strong judgment and good instincts. They seek diverse perspectives and work to disconfirm their beliefs.
interview_probes: Tell me about a time you made a key decision with limited data
Describe a time your judgment was wrong — what did you learn?

leadership_principles.name: Learn and Be Curious
leadership_principles.description: Leaders are never done learning and always seek to improve themselves.
interview_probes: How do you stay current in your field?
Tell me about something new you learned recently that changed your approach
negative_signals: Not curious about how things work

leadership_principles.name: Hire and Develop the Best
leadership_principles.description: Leaders raise the performance bar with every hire and promotion.
interview_probes: Tell me about a time you mentored or developed someone
How do you evaluate talent?

leadership_principles.name: Insist on the Highest Standards
leadership_principles.description: Leaders have relentlessly high standards. They continually raise the bar.
interview_probes: Tell me about a time you refused to accept a mediocre outcome
How do you define quality in your work?

leadership_principles.name: Think Big
leadership_principles.description: Leaders create and communicate a bold direction that inspires results.
interview_probes: Tell me about your most ambitious project
Describe a time you proposed something others thought was too big

leadership_principles.name: Bias for Action
leadership_principles.description: Speed matters in business. Many decisions and actions are reversible and do not need extensive study.
interview_probes: Tell me about a time you made a decision without all the data you wanted
Describe a time when taking quick action was critical

leadership_principles.name: Frugality
leadership_principles.description: Accomplish more with less. Constraints breed resourcefulness, self-sufficiency, and invention.
interview_probes: Tell me about a time you accomplished a big result with limited resources
How do you prioritize when you can't do everything?

leadership_principles.name: Earn Trust
leadership_principles.description: Leaders listen attentively, speak candidly, and treat others respectfully.
interview_probes: Tell me about a time you had to rebuild trust with a colleague
Describe a time you gave difficult feedback

leadership_principles.name: Dive Deep
leadership_principles.description: Leaders operate at all levels, stay connected to the details, audit frequently, and are skeptical when metrics and anecdotes differ.
interview_probes: Tell me about a time you found a problem by diving into the details
Describe a time metrics told a different story than what people were saying

leadership_principles.name: Have Backbone; Disagree and Commit
leadership_principles.description: Leaders are obligated to respectfully challenge decisions when they disagree, even when doing so is uncomfortable. Once a decision is made, they commit wholly.
interview_probes: Tell me about a time you disagreed with your manager or team
Describe a time you committed to a decision you didn't agree with
positive_signals: Doesn't sabotage decisions they disagree with

leadership_principles.name: Deliver Results
leadership_principles.description: Leaders focus on the key inputs for their business and deliver them with the right quality and in a timely fashion.
interview_probes: Tell me about the most impactful result you've delivered
Describe a time you had to overcome significant obstacles to deliver
negative_signals: Gives up when things get hard

leadership_principles.name: Strive to be Earth's Best Employer
leadership_principles.description: Leaders work to create a safer, more productive, more diverse, and more just work environment.
interview_probes: How do you create an inclusive team environment?
Tell me about a time you advocated for a team member

leadership_principles.name: Success and Scale Bring Broad Responsibility
leadership_principles.description: Leaders create more than they consume and always leave things better than they found them.
interview_probes: Tell me about a time you considered the broader impact of your work
How do you think about the societal impact of technology?
